ROI Analyses Hippocampus - Model 1 (Encode & Recall)

Libraries and Functions

# Clear environment
rm(list = ls())

# Get Libraries and Functions
source("~/Dropbox (Personal)/TI_paper/ti-paper/Data/Functions.R")

Import data

setwd("~/Dropbox (Personal)/TI_paper/ti-paper/Data/MRI/FaceNameTask/ROI/Hippocampus")
ROIdata <- read.csv("BOLD_Hipp_model1.csv")

# Assign factors
ROIdata$S_ID <- factor(ROIdata$S_ID)
ROIdata$ID <- factor(ROIdata$ID)
ROIdata$ROI <- factor(ROIdata$ROI)
ROIdata$hemisphere <- factor(ROIdata$hemisphere)
ROIdata$StimType <- factor(ROIdata$StimType)
ROIdata$TaskStage <- factor(ROIdata$TaskStage)

Sham Condition - Hippocampus - Figure 2F

# Does the hippocampus show different activity between encode and recall stages of the task?
# BOLD = median % BOLD signal

Hip_data_Sham <- subset(ROIdata, ROI=="Hippocampus" & StimType=="Sham")

# One sample t-test 
upper = 0.3
type = "two.sided"
Sham_ost <- ddply(Hip_data_Sham, .(TaskStage, hemisphere), t.test.plyr, "BOLD")
Sham_ost <- ost_FDR(Sham_ost)
pander(Sham_ost)
Table continues below
TaskStage hemisphere statistic p.value
Encode L 3.69812064228076 0.00152626851940014
Encode R 3.9214807605266 0.000916710999101395
Recall L -1.28120701566625 0.215538142368785
Recall R -0.250312947945414 0.805032026471034
Table continues below
estimate conf.int1 conf.int2 nobs dof
0.13757115 0.0597100540555932 0.215432245944407 20 19
0.15196645 0.070856926649865 0.233075973350135 20 19
-0.0593915 -0.156415506743164 0.0376325067431636 20 19
-0.015651 -0.146518858592309 0.115216858592309 20 19
method alternative null.value sig FDR
One Sample t-test two.sided 0 ** 0.003053
One Sample t-test two.sided 0 ** 0.003053
One Sample t-test two.sided 0 NA 0.2874
One Sample t-test two.sided 0 NA 0.805 
# LMM - effect of task stage and hemishere on BOLD signal
BA <- lmer(BOLD ~ TaskStage*hemisphere + (1|S_ID), data = Hip_data_Sham, REML = TRUE) 
Anova(BA, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##                            F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## TaskStage            20.4921  1     57 3.097e-05 ***
## hemisphere            0.5211  1     57    0.4733    
## TaskStage:hemisphere  0.1328  1     57    0.7169    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(BA, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: BOLD ~ TaskStage * hemisphere + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: Hip_data_Sham
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -15.4
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.93762 -0.45896 -0.00748  0.39611  2.48798 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.01229  0.1109  
##  Residual             0.03243  0.1801  
## Number of obs: 80, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                             Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)                  0.13757    0.04729 61.96442   2.909  0.00502 **
## TaskStageRecall             -0.19696    0.05695 57.00000  -3.459  0.00103 **
## hemisphereR                  0.01440    0.05695 57.00000   0.253  0.80135   
## TaskStageRecall:hemisphereR  0.02935    0.08054 57.00000   0.364  0.71693   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) TskStR hmsphR
## TaskStgRcll -0.602              
## hemisphereR -0.602  0.500       
## TskStgRcl:R  0.426 -0.707 -0.707
estimated_means <- emmeans(BA, ~ TaskStage*hemisphere)
estimated_means
##  TaskStage hemisphere  emmean     SE df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Encode    L           0.1376 0.0473 62   0.0430   0.2321
##  Recall    L          -0.0594 0.0473 62  -0.1539   0.0351
##  Encode    R           0.1520 0.0473 62   0.0574   0.2465
##  Recall    R          -0.0157 0.0473 62  -0.1102   0.0789
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
# Plot
p.Sham_hipp <-ggplot(Hip_data_Sham, aes(x = hemisphere, y = BOLD, fill=hemisphere)) + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', fill="#56B4E9", stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BOLD (% signal change)",
                           breaks = seq(-.8, .8, .4),
                           limits=c(-1, 1)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
        panel.background = element_blank()) + 
        theme(legend.title=element_blank(), legend.position="none") +
        scale_colour_manual(values=c("#999999", "#999999"))+
        scale_fill_manual(values=c("#999999", "#999999"))  +
        scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("L","R"),
        labels=c("L", "R")) +
        theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) + 
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20), legend.position = "none") + ggtitle("") + xlab("Hippocampus") + facet_grid(.~TaskStage)


ggsave(p.Sham_hipp ,file="Figure_2F.pdf", width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "pdf")   

p.Sham_hipp

Sham Condition - segmented Hippocampus - Figure 2G

## Does the left hippocampus shows different activity during encode across hippocampal regions (anterior, mid, posterior)?

Left_Seg_Hip_data_Sham <- subset(ROIdata, (ROI=="Ant" | ROI=="Mid" | ROI=="Post") & StimType=="Sham" & hemisphere=="L" & TaskStage=="Encode")
BA_seg <- lmer(BOLD ~ ROI + (1|S_ID), data = Left_Seg_Hip_data_Sham, REML = TRUE)
Anova(BA_seg, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##          F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## ROI 8.7171  2     38 0.0007658 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(BA_seg, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: BOLD ~ ROI + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: Left_Seg_Hip_data_Sham
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -46.9
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.8325 -0.5095 -0.1110  0.4632  1.9199 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.02966  0.1722  
##  Residual             0.01034  0.1017  
## Number of obs: 60, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##             Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.20153    0.04472 27.14422   4.506 0.000114 ***
## ROIMid      -0.12938    0.03215 38.00000  -4.024 0.000263 ***
## ROIPost     -0.09569    0.03215 38.00000  -2.976 0.005052 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##         (Intr) ROIMid
## ROIMid  -0.359       
## ROIPost -0.359  0.500
BA_contrasts <- emmeans(BA_seg, specs = pairwise ~ ROI, type = "response")
BA_contrasts
## $emmeans
##  ROI  emmean     SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Ant  0.2015 0.0447 27.1   0.1098    0.293
##  Mid  0.0721 0.0447 27.1  -0.0196    0.164
##  Post 0.1058 0.0447 27.1   0.0141    0.198
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast   estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Ant - Mid    0.1294 0.0322 38   4.024  0.0008
##  Ant - Post   0.0957 0.0322 38   2.976  0.0137
##  Mid - Post  -0.0337 0.0322 38  -1.048  0.5518
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
# Create plot 
p.BA_LHipp_Sham <- ggplot(Left_Seg_Hip_data_Sham, aes(x = ROI, y = BOLD, fill = ROI))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', fill="#56B4E9", stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BOLD (% signal change)",
                           breaks = seq(-.8, 1, .4),
                           limits=c(-.4, 1.2)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank()) +
        scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Ant","Mid", "Post"),
        labels=c("Ant","Mid", "Post")) +
        scale_fill_manual(values=c("#ce4a30", "#56B4E9","#edb047")) +
        theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) +
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 14), legend.position = "none") + xlab("Left Hippocampus")



ggsave(p.BA_LHipp_Sham ,file="Figure_2G.pdf", width = 7, height = 8, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "pdf")
p.BA_LHipp_Sham

## What about the right hippocampus?
Right_Seg_Hip_data_Sham <- subset(ROIdata, (ROI=="Ant" | ROI=="Mid" | ROI=="Post") & StimType=="Sham" & hemisphere=="R" & TaskStage=="Encode")
BA_seg <- lmer(BOLD ~ ROI + (1|S_ID), data = Right_Seg_Hip_data_Sham, REML = TRUE)
Anova(BA_seg, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##          F Df Df.res Pr(>F)
## ROI 0.1991  2     38 0.8203
# no ROI effect for the right hippocampus

Stim Conditions - whole Hippocampus - Figures 3B and 3G

# Does stimulation impact BOLD activity in the targeted hippocampus - Left ?

Hip_data_L<- subset(ROIdata, ROI=="Hippocampus" & hemisphere=="L")
BA <- lmer(BOLD ~ StimType*TaskStage + (1|S_ID), data = Hip_data_L, REML = TRUE)
Anova(BA, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##                          F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## StimType            3.2224  2     95   0.04425 *  
## TaskStage          44.8436  1     95 1.492e-09 ***
## StimType:TaskStage  2.9611  2     95   0.05656 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(BA, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: BOLD ~ StimType * TaskStage + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: Hip_data_L
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -81.2
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.53487 -0.51943 -0.00584  0.58444  2.95468 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.01817  0.1348  
##  Residual             0.01767  0.1329  
## Number of obs: 120, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                     0.13757    0.04233 49.88460   3.250  0.00207
## StimTypeTI 1:1                 -0.01244    0.04203 95.00000  -0.296  0.76792
## StimTypeTI 1:3                 -0.13167    0.04203 95.00000  -3.133  0.00230
## TaskStageRecall                -0.19696    0.04203 95.00000  -4.686 9.29e-06
## StimTypeTI 1:1:TaskStageRecall -0.01420    0.05944 95.00000  -0.239  0.81175
## StimTypeTI 1:3:TaskStageRecall  0.11757    0.05944 95.00000   1.978  0.05084
##                                   
## (Intercept)                    ** 
## StimTypeTI 1:1                    
## StimTypeTI 1:3                 ** 
## TaskStageRecall                ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1:TaskStageRecall    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:TaskStageRecall .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 TskStR STTI1:1:
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.496                                  
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.496  0.500                           
## TaskStgRcll -0.496  0.500    0.500                  
## STTI1:1:TSR  0.351 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707         
## STTI1:3:TSR  0.351 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707  0.500
BA_contrasts <- emmeans(BA, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|TaskStage, type = "response")
BA_contrasts
## $emmeans
## TaskStage = Encode:
##  StimType  emmean     SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham      0.1376 0.0423 49.9   0.0525   0.2226
##  TI 1:1    0.1251 0.0423 49.9   0.0401   0.2102
##  TI 1:3    0.0059 0.0423 49.9  -0.0791   0.0909
## 
## TaskStage = Recall:
##  StimType  emmean     SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham     -0.0594 0.0423 49.9  -0.1444   0.0256
##  TI 1:1   -0.0860 0.0423 49.9  -0.1711  -0.0010
##  TI 1:3   -0.0735 0.0423 49.9  -0.1585   0.0115
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## TaskStage = Encode:
##  contrast        estimate    SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0124 0.042 95   0.296  0.9529
##  Sham - TI 1:3     0.1317 0.042 95   3.133  0.0065
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3   0.1192 0.042 95   2.837  0.0153
## 
## TaskStage = Recall:
##  contrast        estimate    SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0266 0.042 95   0.634  0.8020
##  Sham - TI 1:3     0.0141 0.042 95   0.335  0.9399
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.0125 0.042 95  -0.298  0.9522
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
## Plot - Figure 2K

p.BA.hipp_L <- ggplot(Hip_data_L, aes(x = StimType, y = BOLD, fill = StimType))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BOLD (% signal change)",
                           breaks = seq(-.8, 1, .4),
                           limits=c(-.6, 1.2)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 45, face = "bold", hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
            legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank()) +
        scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Sham","TI 1:1","TI 1:3"),
        labels=c("Sham","TI 1:1","TI 1:3")) +
        theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) +
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20), legend.position = "none") + ggtitle("") + xlab("Left Hippocampus") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"))  + facet_grid(.~TaskStage)


ggsave(p.BA.hipp_L,file="Figure_3B.pdf", width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "pdf")


p.BA.hipp_L

# What about the right hippocampus, not targeted?

Hip_data_R<- subset(ROIdata, ROI=="Hippocampus" & hemisphere=="R")
BA <- lmer(BOLD ~ StimType*TaskStage + (1|S_ID), data = Hip_data_R, REML = TRUE)
Anova(BA, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##                          F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## StimType            0.7244  2     95    0.4873    
## TaskStage          18.3715  1     95 4.359e-05 ***
## StimType:TaskStage  2.0744  2     95    0.1313    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(BA, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: BOLD ~ StimType * TaskStage + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: Hip_data_R
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -27.7
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.48440 -0.49093  0.06146  0.55882  2.70726 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.02132  0.1460  
##  Residual             0.02970  0.1723  
## Number of obs: 120, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)                     0.15197    0.05051 60.86466   3.009  0.00381 **
## StimTypeTI 1:1                 -0.01979    0.05450 95.00000  -0.363  0.71727   
## StimTypeTI 1:3                 -0.10629    0.05450 95.00000  -1.950  0.05409 . 
## TaskStageRecall                -0.16762    0.05450 95.00000  -3.076  0.00274 **
## StimTypeTI 1:1:TaskStageRecall -0.02407    0.07708 95.00000  -0.312  0.75549   
## StimTypeTI 1:3:TaskStageRecall  0.12231    0.07708 95.00000   1.587  0.11585   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 TskStR STTI1:1:
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.540                                  
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.540  0.500                           
## TaskStgRcll -0.540  0.500    0.500                  
## STTI1:1:TSR  0.381 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707         
## STTI1:3:TSR  0.381 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707  0.500
BA_contrasts <- emmeans(BA, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|TaskStage, type = "response")
BA_contrasts
## $emmeans
## TaskStage = Encode:
##  StimType   emmean     SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham      0.15197 0.0505 60.9   0.0510   0.2530
##  TI 1:1    0.13217 0.0505 60.9   0.0312   0.2332
##  TI 1:3    0.04567 0.0505 60.9  -0.0553   0.1467
## 
## TaskStage = Recall:
##  StimType   emmean     SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham     -0.01565 0.0505 60.9  -0.1167   0.0854
##  TI 1:1   -0.05952 0.0505 60.9  -0.1605   0.0415
##  TI 1:3    0.00037 0.0505 60.9  -0.1006   0.1014
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## TaskStage = Encode:
##  contrast        estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0198 0.0545 95   0.363  0.9299
##  Sham - TI 1:3     0.1063 0.0545 95   1.950  0.1304
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3   0.0865 0.0545 95   1.587  0.2562
## 
## TaskStage = Recall:
##  contrast        estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0439 0.0545 95   0.805  0.7009
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.0160 0.0545 95  -0.294  0.9535
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.0599 0.0545 95  -1.099  0.5172
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
## Plot - Figure 4L
p.BA.hipp_R <- ggplot(Hip_data_R, aes(x = StimType, y = BOLD, fill = StimType))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BOLD (% signal change)",
                           breaks = seq(-.8, 1, .4),
                           limits=c(-.6, 1.2)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 45, face = "bold", hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
            legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank()) +
        scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Sham","TI 1:1","TI 1:3"),
        labels=c("Sham","TI 1:1","TI 1:3")) +
        theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) +
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20), legend.position = "none") + ggtitle("") + xlab("Right Hippocampus") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"))  + facet_grid(.~TaskStage)



ggsave(p.BA.hipp_R,file="Figure_3G.pdf", width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "pdf")

p.BA.hipp_R

Stim Conditions - segmented Left Hippocampus - Figure 3C

# Effect of stimulation was seen only for the left hippocampus - Does it affect segments of the hippocampus differently?

Seg_Hip_data_enc_L <- subset(ROIdata, (ROI=="Ant" | ROI=="Mid" | ROI=="Post") & TaskStage=="Encode" &  hemisphere=="L")
BA_seg <- lmer(BOLD ~ StimType*ROI + (1|S_ID), data = Seg_Hip_data_enc_L, REML = TRUE)
Anova(BA_seg, test="F") 
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: BOLD
##                    F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## StimType     12.6459  2    152 8.313e-06 ***
## ROI           6.3506  2    152  0.002245 ** 
## StimType:ROI  0.4639  4    152  0.762166    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(BA_seg, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: BOLD ~ StimType * ROI + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: Seg_Hip_data_enc_L
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -63.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.84114 -0.67097  0.02941  0.56009  2.57765 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.02302  0.1517  
##  Residual             0.02715  0.1648  
## Number of obs: 180, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                          Estimate Std. Error         df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)              0.201525   0.050085  63.693682   4.024 0.000155 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1          -0.008413   0.052103 152.000000  -0.161 0.871932    
## StimTypeTI 1:3          -0.176966   0.052103 152.000000  -3.396 0.000871 ***
## ROIMid                  -0.129385   0.052103 152.000000  -2.483 0.014106 *  
## ROIPost                 -0.095693   0.052103 152.000000  -1.837 0.068222 .  
## StimTypeTI 1:1:ROIMid    0.020860   0.073685 152.000000   0.283 0.777487    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:ROIMid    0.085673   0.073685 152.000000   1.163 0.246776    
## StimTypeTI 1:1:ROIPost  -0.026089   0.073685 152.000000  -0.354 0.723779    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:ROIPost   0.037827   0.073685 152.000000   0.513 0.608446    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##              (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 ROIMid ROIPst STTI1:1:ROIM STTI1:3:ROIM
## StmTypTI1:1  -0.520                                                          
## StmTypTI1:3  -0.520  0.500                                                   
## ROIMid       -0.520  0.500    0.500                                          
## ROIPost      -0.520  0.500    0.500    0.500                                 
## STTI1:1:ROIM  0.368 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707 -0.354                          
## STTI1:3:ROIM  0.368 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707 -0.354  0.500                   
## STTI1:1:ROIP  0.368 -0.707   -0.354   -0.354 -0.707  0.500        0.250      
## STTI1:3:ROIP  0.368 -0.354   -0.707   -0.354 -0.707  0.250        0.500      
##              STTI1:1:ROIP
## StmTypTI1:1              
## StmTypTI1:3              
## ROIMid                   
## ROIPost                  
## STTI1:1:ROIM             
## STTI1:3:ROIM             
## STTI1:1:ROIP             
## STTI1:3:ROIP  0.500
##
##
emmip(BA_seg, StimType~ROI)

estimated_means <- emmeans(BA_seg, ~ StimType*ROI)


# Plot
p.BA_seg_enc.L <- ggplot(Seg_Hip_data_enc_L, aes(x = ROI, y = BOLD, fill = StimType)) +
       stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "BOLD (% signal change)",
                           breaks = seq(-.8, 1, .4),
                           limits=c(-.8, 1.2)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold", hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank())  + 
        scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Ant","Mid", "Post"),
        labels=c("Ant","Mid", "Post")) +
        theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) + 
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
          legend.text = element_text(size=14),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.5, 'cm')) + ggtitle("") + xlab("Left Hippocampus")  + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"), name = "", labels = c("Sham", "TI 1:1", "TI 1:3"))

ggsave(p.BA_seg_enc.L,file="Figure_3C.pdf", width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "pdf")

p.BA_seg_enc.L
